mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate signals easing financial conditions and a lower discount rate for equities, so S&P 500 returns turn positive over the next month when policy is actively being cut.
failure_condition: If 20-day forward SPX returns are not higher than baseline after FEDFUNDS declines over a rolling 3-month window, or if triggered periods produce negative median returns, reject the hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above its recent trend signals labor-market deterioration and impending earnings weakness, so equity prices should fall over the next 20 trading days once unemployment breaks out.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if unemployment-breakout episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the median forward return is not below the unconditional median.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized drop in RSAFS and HOUST captures weakening household and goods demand, which should pressure cyclicals and the index level, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: If joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST do not improve downside prediction versus either series alone, or if 60-day forward SPX returns are positive in triggered instances more than half the time, reject.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO indicates industrial output contraction and weaker corporate revenue momentum, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns remain positive after month-over-month INDPRO declines, or if the signal loses directional accuracy versus baseline over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp fall in GDPC1 signals a macro demand shock severe enough to reduce earnings expectations and risk appetite, so the index should continue lower over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 contracts on the latest quarterly release versus the prior quarter
failure_condition: Reject if quarterly GDP contractions are not followed by negative 60-day forward SPX returns more often than not, or if the conditional median return is indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS declines while UNRATE rises, the labor market is simultaneously losing jobs and slack is increasing, which should create a strong negative equity signal over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-down/UNRATE-up state does not outperform each single proxy in predicting negative 20-day SPX returns, or if positive returns dominate in that state.

mechanism_narrative: A CPIAUCSL inflation pickup occurring while FEDFUNDS is still low implies real rates remain easy and policy stays accommodative, which should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above its 6-month trend while FEDFUNDS stays below 2%
regime_hint: early inflation pickup with accommodative policy
failure_condition: Reject if inflation-pickup episodes under low FEDFUNDS are followed by negative 20-day SPX returns more often than positive ones, or if the joint interaction adds no lift over CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: During a manufacturing recession, a recovery in INDPRO indicates output stabilization before earnings estimates fully reset, so equity returns should turn positive over the next quarter once industrial production stops falling.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO turns positive month-over-month after at least two consecutive monthly declines
failure_condition: Reject if INDPRO rebound episodes do not precede positive 60-day SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the effect disappears when UNRATE is not elevated.
